It is classified as a member of the family Orchidaceae, which comprises the vast majority of orchid species. The Selenipedium genus is further classified within the subfamily Cypripedioideae. intro: Selenipedium × coppinianum, also known as the Venus slipper orchid, is a hybrid plant species within the genus Selenipedium, characterized by its unique, slipper-shaped flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Selenipedium × coppinianum include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Selenipedium × coppinianum is typically used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often employed to alleviate respiratory symptoms and promote relaxation.
